name: CROMER RES (KENNETH)
address: 2777 NW THREE SISTERS DRIVE, BEND, OR 97703

fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Patio Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on the property could be achieved by addressing the dense vegetation, which may obstruct responder access and visibility. Strategically trimming or thinning the landscaping could improve sightlines and facilitate easier navigation for responders approaching from various directions. Additionally, increasing access points or expanding the existing ones around the rear of the property may enhance route flexibility and allow for more efficient emergency operations. Ensuring that pathways are clearly marked and easily traversable may support effective staging and movement throughout the area. This approach could assist emergency personnel in quickly and safely reaching key locations such as the main building, roof system, and patio area, thereby potentially improving response times and operational success.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Tree Cluster; Open Terrain; Defensible Space; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Fuel Continuity
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by increasing separation between vegetation and structural components and reducing vegetation continuity near critical areas. Thinning or selectively removing scattered shrubs and trees, especially those in clusters, may help reduce combustible fuel concentrations and support wildfire management efforts. Maintaining open terrain can serve as a valuable buffer, so efforts should focus on preserving these areas while ensuring they remain clear and accessible. Managing the vegetated perimeter by ensuring regular maintenance and reducing dense vegetation near structures can help minimize potential fire spread. By focusing on fuel continuity reduction and managing vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure, the property could improve its capacity to withstand wildfire exposure while supporting firefighter access and suppression efforts. Overall, these actions can contribute to better property preparedness and long-term wildfire resilience.
